message:
You know that one friend who always seems to panic at the slightest hint of trouble? Yep, I have one of those too. The other day, we were chatting about emergency preparedness plans over coffee, and she was convinced that we were all just one small earthquake away from total chaos. 

Instead of rolling my eyes, I decided to take a more practical approach. I shared how my family and I have our go-bags packed and a simple plan in place. It’s not about being alarmist; it’s just about being ready without the stress. 

By the end of our chat, she felt a little more at ease and actually started thinking about what she might want to include in her own go-bag. It was a nice reminder that preparedness doesn’t have to be overwhelming; it can just be a little everyday planning. 

Have you ever had a friend who tends to jump to the worst-case scenario? How do you help them find that calm? This little refresher helped us both!
